arts.ttl: Newcastle v Fulham line-ups: Traore replaces Wilson
arts.sum: Adama Traore starts for Fulham at St James’ Park in place of the injured Harry Wilson. Wilson faces a lengthy spell on the sidelines with a foot injury. The home side, meanwhile, are unchanged. Newcastle: Dubravka; Livramento, Schar, Burn, Hall; Guimaraes, Tonali, Joelinton; Murphy, Isak, Gordon. Subs: Pope, Trippier, Kelly, Krafth, Botman, Miley, Willock, Longstaff, […] The post Newcastle v Fulham line-ups: Traore replaces Wilson appeared first on West London Sport.

arts.ttl: Bournemouth v Liverpool, Newcastle v Fulham, and more: football – live
arts.sum: Updates from the Premier League and further afield

arts.ttl: Newcastle United transfer blow as Marcus Rashford move sends alarm bells ringing after £71m twist
arts.sum: One of their major threats will be Aston Villa, who enjoyed a similar season to Newcastle last season by qualifying for the Champions League after a fourth-placed finish. Newcastle United's hopes of qualifying for next season's Champions League are not just dependent on what they do, but also how their rivals fare in the last four months of the season.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United receive new enquiry for Lloyd Kelly as three clubs express late interest
arts.sum: But Newcastle are unlikely to do any of their rivals any favours in the remainder of the window meaning the Serie A club could be in with a shout of they put something spectacular together. Lloyd Kelly is the subject of fresh and late transfer window interest from Serie A side Juventus.

arts.ttl: How to watch Newcastle vs Fulham - TV channel, live stream details
arts.sum: Newcastle United will look to continue their quest for Champions League qualification when they host Fulham this weekend. But Fulham, on the other hand, have their own dreams of European qualification this season and will be looking to spoil the party at St James' Park on Saturday afternoon.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United face truth over 2025 summer war chest as new £40million reality emerges
arts.sum: Newcastle United boss Eddie Howe must wait to discover the truth about how much he will get to spend in the next transfer window - but the head coach is not understood to be expecting any type of warchest. Chronicle Live was told again on Friday evening that Newcastle would not be placing any huge bids for players in the lead-up to deadline day with Howe stating on the eve of the Fulham game that business was "done".

arts.ttl: Eddie Howe blocking Newcastle United exit amid late transfer and loan interest
arts.sum: However, with Callum Wilson still not back to full fitness and Howe's attacking options depleted after Miguel Almiron's exit, the Magpies chief wants to keep Osula at the club to play backup to Alexander Isak. After just eight league appearances this term for the backup striker - with just 41 minutes played in total - loan enquiries have been made for the 21-year-old, with Austrian side Sturm Graz said to be keen on Newcastle United's summer signing.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United in negotiations with Real Betis – Winter move from Magpies ‘gaining strength’
arts.sum: Newcastle United’s William Osula has emerged as a new target for Real Betis late in the winter market. Real Betis are working on bringing in a striker to provide Manuel Pellegrini options in the attack for the second half of the season. Their failure to secure other targets has seen them now shift their focus The post Newcastle United in negotiations with Real Betis – Winter move from Magpies ‘gaining strength’ appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Fulham take ‘most concrete steps’ for signing – Marco Silva’s side ready to bring player to PL
arts.sum: Fulham are keen on signing AS Roma’s Matías Soulé before the winter market closes on Monday. That’s according to Italian journalist Gianluca Di Marzio, and he now brings the latest on Fulham’s plan to try and sign the 21-year-old. arts.ttl: Alan Shearer turns air blue after Howard Webb insult as storm hit Newcastle United 'scandal'
arts.sum: On an afternoon in which the Magpies - led by Alan Shearer - could have scraped to survival with just a point, things could have been very different had things not unfolded in such a controversial manner just seven days earlier at St James' Park. As Fulham make the trip to St James' Park today to take on Eddie Howe's side for a crunch clash, it stirs memories of when Shearer and Newcastle were completely robbed in their home game against Fulham on the penultimate weekend of the ill-fated 2008/09 season - a game that had so much riding on it.
